name: go-git-it
description: Version control for vibe-coders — no Git jargon, just friendly snapshots.

walkthroughs.title: Get started with Go Git It
walkthroughs.description: Learn how to save your work, back it up, and experiment safely.

steps.title: Taking your first snapshot
steps.description:
A snapshot saves your work at this exact moment — like a save point in a game. Take one whenever you finish something that's working.
[Take a Snapshot](command:go-git-it.takeSnapshot)

steps.title: Setting up GitHub (one time)
steps.description:
Before you can back up online, you need two free things: a GitHub account and the GitHub CLI.

[Install the GitHub CLI](https://cli.github.com)
[Create a free GitHub account](https://github.com/signup)

steps.title: Connecting and backing up to GitHub
steps.description:
Once you're set up, click Connect to GitHub in the sidebar — Go Git It creates the repo and uploads your work in one step.

[Connect to GitHub](command:go-git-it.connectToGitHub)
[Send to GitHub](command:go-git-it.pushToGitHub)

steps.title: Starting an experiment
steps.description:
Try risky ideas without fear. Experiments are completely separate from your main work — you can always abandon them.
[Start an Experiment](command:go-git-it.startExperiment)

steps.title: Coming back if something breaks
steps.description: The timeline on the left shows every snapshot you've taken. Click any snapshot to go back to that moment.

walkthroughs.title: Demo: Go Git It Walkthrough
walkthroughs.description: A complete start-to-finish example — from creating a project to experimenting and rewinding.

steps.title: Step 1 — Start or open a project
steps.description:
Every project needs a home. You can build a brand new one or open something you've already been working on.

[🏗️ Build a new project](command:go-git-it.buildNewProject)
[📂 Open a different project](command:go-git-it.openDifferentProject)

steps.title: Step 2 — Take your first snapshot
steps.description:
You've set up your project and written some code. Time to save your progress.

[📸 Take a snapshot](command:go-git-it.takeSnapshot)

steps.title: Step 3 — Connect and back up to GitHub
steps.description:
Your snapshot is saved on your computer. Now back it up online so it's safe no matter what.

First time? You'll need the GitHub CLI set up — see the main walkthrough for a 2-minute guide.

[🔗 Connect to GitHub](command:go-git-it.connectToGitHub)
[☁️ Send to GitHub](command:go-git-it.pushToGitHub)

steps.title: Step 4 — Keep working and snapshotting
steps.description:
Build more features. Take a snapshot whenever something is working. Think of it as saving your game.

[📸 Take a snapshot](command:go-git-it.takeSnapshot)

steps.title: Step 5 — Try something risky with an experiment
steps.description:
You want to try a big redesign but don't want to break what's working. Start an experiment.

[🧪 Start a new experiment](command:go-git-it.startExperiment)

steps.title: Step 6 — Work inside the experiment
steps.description:
Make changes, take snapshots, break things. Your main line is completely untouched.

[📸 Take a snapshot](command:go-git-it.takeSnapshot)

steps.title: Step 7 — Finish or abandon the experiment
steps.description:
The experiment worked — merge it in. Or it didn't — abandon it. Either way, your main work is safe.

[✅ Finish this experiment](command:go-git-it.finishExperiment)
[🗑️ Abandon this experiment](command:go-git-it.abandonExperiment)

steps.title: Step 8 — Go back if something breaks
steps.description: Something went wrong. No problem — click any dot in the timeline to rewind to that moment.
